title: Reported Speech with Imperatives
objectives: Apply the structure of reported speech with imperatives using to / not to accurately
Produce reported speech sentences correctly when transforming direct commands
Demonstrate accuracy when rewriting commands in reported speech

key_concepts.explanation: We use reported speech when we want to tell someone what another person said. Form: subject + reporting verb + object + (to / not to) + base verb. Use 'to + verb' for positive commands. Use 'not to + verb' for negative commands.
told → to give a direct instruction. asked → to make a polite request. advised → to give helpful advice. warned → to give a strong warning.

Direct: 'Drink water before you start running,' said the coach. → Reported: The coach told the players to drink water before they started running. Direct: 'Don't stay up too late,' said my father. → Reported: My father warned me not to stay up too late. Direct: 'Take your jacket. It's cold outside,' said my friend. → Reported: My friend advised me to take my jacket because it was cold outside. Direct: 'Please carry this box for me,' asked my mother. → Reported: My mother asked me to carry that box for her.
Don't change the tense in the reported imperatives. The base verb stays the same.

solution_steps: The mother told her son to use his phone for learning.
examples.answer: The mother told her son to use his phone for learning.

solution_steps: The teacher told them not to share their passwords.
examples.answer: The teacher told them not to share their passwords.

exercises.problem: Complete: 1. 'Please close the door quietly,' asked my brother. → My brother ___ my sister ___ the door quietly. 2. 'Plan your time before exams,' said the teacher. → The teacher ___ the students ___ their time before exams.
exercises.answer: 1. asked, to close. 2. told, to plan.

exercises.problem: Rewrite the commands using reported speech: 1. 'Don't forget to feed the cat.' → My mother asked me ___. 2. 'Pass the ball to your teammate.' → The coach ___. 3. 'Don't eat a lot of sweets,' said the doctor. → ___.
exercises.answer: 1. My mother asked me not to forget to feed the cat. 2. The coach told me/him to pass the ball to my/his teammate. 3. The doctor advised me/him not to eat a lot of sweets.

exercises.problem: Say it Again: Work in pairs. Think of commands from daily life (home/school). Give the commands to your partner. Your partner reports them back. Switch turns. Example: A: 'Bring your book.' B: My friend told me to bring my book.

teaching_notes: Grammar lesson on reported speech with imperatives. Form: subject + reporting verb + object + to/not to + base verb. Four reporting verbs: told, asked, advised, warned. Grammar Box table with direct → reported examples. Key tip: don't change tense in reported imperatives. Matching activity with pictures (mother, father, IT expert, teacher). Practice: controlled completion → rewriting → free pair practice. 1 page.
